channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Toyota Introduces 2026 Land Cruiser Prado with Improved Performance, Hybrid Powertrain, and Advanced Safety Tech
item.description: Toyota has rolled out the 2026 Land Cruiser Prado, which brings back its rugged off-road heritage while embracing modern design and hybrid technology. The new Prado, also known as the LC250, is built ...

item.title: Tanzania equips social welfare officers with smartphones to enhance service delivery
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has taken a major step towards modernising social welfare services by equipping Social Welfare Officers in all 26 mainland regions with smartphones. The initiative, led by M...

item.title: Gwadar Port marks key step as specialised transhipment vessel berths
item.description: Federal Minister for Maritime Affairs Junaid Anwar Chaudhry has described the berthing of a specialised vessel at Gwadar Port as a practical sign of the port’s growing operational role in regional shi...

item.title: Intensifying Iran Conflict Brings Regional Industries Under Fire
item.description: The conflict between Iran and the US-Israel alliance has sharply escalated, with strikes exchanged on industrial, military, and media targets across the Middle East. Iran launched missiles and drones ...

item.title: Pakistan unveils hybrid strategy to manage soaring summer power demand amid fuel shortages
item.description: The Dawn report outlines Pakistan’s plan to address a looming summer power crunch amid disruptions in LNG, coal, and gas supplies. Government officials say the strategy combines load-shedding, mandato...

item.title: Regular Oil Shipments Arrive in Pakistan as Hormuz Route Reopens
item.description: In a positive development for Pakistan’s energy supply, more oil and chemical tankers have continued to reach the country through the Strait of Hormuz following Iran’s approval for Pakistani vessels t...

item.title: Family Declines to Bury Estranged Father After 26 Years of Separation
item.description: A man from Morogoro, Tanzania, identified as Issa Maulid, passed away shortly after seeking forgiveness from the family he had abandoned 26 years earlier. In a heartfelt video shared on the Binti Kigo...

item.title: Kenyan Man Found Guilty of Massive US Fraud Faces Deportation
item.description: A Kenyan man living in the United States has been ordered to be deported after being found guilty of taking part in a multi-billion shilling scam that defrauded individuals and businesses of about Sh1...

item.title: Kenya and Uganda urged to speed up Afcon 2027 preparations amid readiness concerns
item.description: Kenya and Uganda, who are co-hosting the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon) together with Tanzania, are facing pressure to quicken their preparations as time runs out. With only about one year left be...

item.title: Expanding school meals could help keep learners in class
item.description: The editorial calls for the government and development partners to strengthen Kenya’s school feeding programme as a key way to reduce dropout rates, especially in drought-prone and low-income areas. T...

item.title: Curb wasteful spending on government office renovations
item.description: In this opinion piece, columnist Kaltum D. Guyo raises concern over the growing trend where top government officials are constantly seeking more funds to refurbish their offices. She points out that t...

item.title: Kenya’s progressive approach to nation-building and governance reform
item.description: The article 'Evolving model of national transformation' explores how Kenya’s development philosophy and governance model have gradually shifted to match new realities in social, political, and economi...

item.title: Kenyan Youths and the Rise of Fraudulent ‘Sharp Boys’ Culture
item.description: The article sheds light on a growing phenomenon among Kenyan youths known as 'sharp boys'—young men who depend on tricks and scams to make money in tough economic times. At first, the phrase suggested...

item.title: Kenya’s Empty Coffers and the Myth of Missing Corruption Money
item.description: This article was meant to be an opinion piece that reflects on Kenya’s current financial situation. From the title, it seems the writer was addressing the common phrase that there’s no money left to s...

item.title: Trump’s Potential Endorsement Could Shape California’s Republican Gubernatorial Race
item.description: The upcoming California governor’s race has become a high-stakes contest where two leading Republicans, Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco and conservative commentator Steve Hilton, are vying for Do...

item.title: Internal Tensions Challenge Raila Odinga’s Standing Within ODM
item.description: The Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), one of Kenya’s largest political parties, is facing internal rifts that could taint the legacy of its long-time leader, Raila Odinga. Reports suggest growing divi...

item.title: Veron Mosengo-Omba steps down as CAF Secretary General following internal challenges in African football
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) is facing another wave of leadership instability after its Secretary General, Veron Mosengo-Omba, stepped down from his position. Mosengo-Omba, who has been...

item.title: Pakistan and Saudi Arabia reaffirm unity and cooperation amid Middle East crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif appreciated Saudi Arabia’s ‘remarkable restraint’ amid the ongoing Middle East crisis during a meeting with Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud in Isl...

item.title: Police allege PSL security protocol breach involving Shaheen Afridi and Sikandar Raza
item.description: According to the report, Lahore Deputy Inspector-General of Police (Operations) Muhammad Faisal informed the Pakistan Super League CEO about an alleged breach of security protocols involving Lahore Qa...

item.title: Human-wildlife conflict in Kilifi leaves Grade 7 pupil dead, schools in fear
item.description: A Grade 7 pupil from a school in Kilifi County lost his life after being attacked by a lion, an incident that has reignited fears about rising human-wildlife conflict along Kenya’s coastal region. Rep...
